heading: Durren Durren vs Dyers Crossing — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Durren Durren, New South Wales and Dyers Crossing, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Durren Durren has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Dyers Crossing (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Durren Durren or Dyers Crossing safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Durren Durren has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Dyers Crossing scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Durren Durren scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Dyers Crossing scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Durren Durren or Dyers Crossing?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Durren Durren has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
